name: Pollard's rho integer factorization
task: Create /tmp/eval_prho/prho.py with pollard_rho(n) returning a non-trivial factor of n (1 < factor < n). For even n return 2. Otherwise use the iteration f(x) = (x² + c) mod n with cycle detection via Floyd's tortoise-and-hare: x = f(x); y = f(f(y)); d = gcd(|x - y|, n). If d hits n, vary c and retry. Cryptanalytic primitive — semiprime n is the typical input.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_prho/prho.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); F = mod.pollard_rho; f = F(8051); assert 8051 % f == 0 and 1 < f < 8051; n = 1000003 * 1000033; f2 = F(n); assert n % f2 == 0 and f2 in (1000003, 1000033); print('OK')
